examples.title: Forward State Space Search Algorithm
examples.description: Basic forward search algorithm for planning - explores state space from initial state toward goal.
examples.explanation: Standard forward search in planning. Uses applicable actions to generate new states until goal is reached. Can use different queueing strategies (BFS, UCS, A*) for different search behaviors.

examples.title: Backward Goal Regression Algorithm
examples.description: Backward search algorithm that uses goal regression to find predecessor states.
examples.explanation: Backward search computes goal regression. Starting from goal, finds what states could have led to it. Only considers relevant actions that could achieve parts of the goal.
examples.timeComplexity: O(b^d) with smaller b than forward

examples.title: Partial Order Planning Algorithm
examples.description: Constraint-based planning algorithm that maintains partial ordering of actions.
examples.explanation: POP builds plans incrementally by selecting actions to satisfy open preconditions. Maintains causal links and ordering constraints. Returns partially ordered plan when all preconditions are supported.

examples.title: Delete-Relaxation Heuristic (h-add)
examples.description: Computes admissible heuristic by ignoring delete effects of actions.
examples.explanation: The h-add heuristic computes cost ignoring delete lists - only counts what's added. It's admissible (never overestimates) because adding more actions can only increase true cost. Used in many modern planners.
